ddls:
CREATE TABLE "effects" (
  "effect_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each observed effect */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the study associated with the effect */,
  "effect_description" TEXT /* Description of the effect observed */,
  "noael" REAL /* No Observed Adverse Effect Level */,
  "noael_units" TEXT /* Units for NOAEL */,
  "loael" REAL /* Lowest Observed Adverse Effect Level */,
  "loael_units" TEXT /* Units for LOAEL */,
  "mean_observed_value" REAL /* Mean observed value of the effect */,
  "mean_observed_units" TEXT /* Units for the mean observed value */,
  "stat_descriptor" TEXT /* Main statistical descriptor used (e.g., SD, SEM) */,
  "descriptor_value" REAL /* Value of the descriptor */,
  "descriptor_units" TEXT /* Units for the descriptor value */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments or notes regarding the effect */,
  "effect_category" TEXT /* The category of the effect (e.g., growth, health, behavior) */,
  "effect_severity" TEXT /* The severity of the effect (e.g., mild, moderate, severe) */,
  "effect_onset" TEXT /* The time of onset of the effect (e.g., immediate, delayed) */,
  "effect_duration" TEXT /* The duration of the effect (e.g., short-term, long-term) */,
  "effect_reversibility" TEXT /* Whether the effect is reversible (e.g., yes, no) */,
  "effect_pvalue" REAL /* The p-value associated with the effect */,
  "effect_confidence_interval" TEXT /* The confidence interval for the effect */,
  "effect_sample_size" INTEGER /* The sample size used to determine the effect */,
  "effect_statistical_test" TEXT /* The statistical test used to analyze the effect */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("effect_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mycotoxins" (
  "mycotoxin_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mycotoxin */,
  "mycotoxin_name" TEXT /* Name of the mycotoxin */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the mycotoxin */,
  "toxicity_level" TEXT /* The toxicity level of the mycotoxin (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "source" TEXT /* The primary source of the mycotoxin (e.g., Fusarium, Aspergillus) */,
  "chemical_formula" TEXT /* The chemical formula of the mycotoxin */,
  "molecular_weight" REAL /* The molecular weight of the mycotoxin */,
  "cas_number" TEXT /* The CAS number for the mycotoxin */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mycotoxin_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "species" (
  "species_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each species */,
  "species_name" TEXT /* Scientific name of the species */,
  "common_name" TEXT /* Common name of the species */,
  "scientific_classification" TEXT /* The scientific classification of the species */,
  "diet_type" TEXT /* The typical diet type of the species (e.g., herbivore, omnivore, carnivore) */,
  "average_lifespan" INTEGER /* The average lifespan of the species in years */,
  "average_body_weight" REAL /* The average body weight of the species in grams */,
  "geographical_distribution" TEXT /* The geographical distribution of the species */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("species_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "strains" (
  "strain_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each strain */,
  "species_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the species the strain belongs to */,
  "strain_name" TEXT /* Name of the strain */,
  "strain_characteristics" TEXT /* Key characteristics of the strain */,
  "strain_origin" TEXT /* The origin of the strain */,
  "strain_usage" TEXT /* Common usage of the strain in research */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("strain_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sex" (
  "sex_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sex category */,
  "sex_description" TEXT /* Description of the sex (e.g., Male, Female) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sex_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "exposure_methods" (
  "method_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each method */,
  "method_name" TEXT /* Name of the exposure method */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the method */,
  "method_type" TEXT /* The type of exposure method (e.g., dietary, direct) */,
  "method_details" TEXT /* Detailed description of the method */,
  "method_risk_level" TEXT /* The risk level associated with the method (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "statistical_descriptors" (
  "descriptor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each descriptor */,
  "descriptor_name" TEXT /* Name of the statistical descriptor (e.g., SD, SEM) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the descriptor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("descriptor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* The department the user belongs to */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* The phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* The address of the user */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* The date the user joined the organization */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* The last date the user logged in */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the study */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the study was accessed */,
  "access_action" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_duration" TEXT /* The duration of the access */,
  "access_device" TEXT /* The device used to access the study (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "access_ip_address" TEXT /* The IP address from which the study was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
